   <media:title>How Are Memories Stored Inside Your Brain?</media:title>
   <media:content url="https://www.youtube.com/v/PqtggjVAi8M?version=3" type="application/x-shockwave-flash" width="640" height="390"/>
   <media:thumbnail url="https://i1.ytimg.com/vi/PqtggjVAi8M/hqdefault.jpg" width="480" height="360"/>
   <media:description>Avoid your blindspots and get every side of every story at https://ground.news/nutshell. Subscribe for 40% off unlimited access.

Brighten your space with our new human-made & high-quality Neon Framed Art Prints: https://shop.kgs.link/faves 💛 Shop these and more of our handpicked favorites in the kurzgesagt shop!

Sources & further reading:
https://sites.google.com/view/sources-memory/

Memory is one of the strangest abilities you possess. Your brain uses an incredibly complex biological system to preserve moments from your past that no longer exist, allowing you to revisit experiences from years ago. But memories are not always recordings of reality. In fact, every time a memory comes under the spotlight of your attention, it can melt and change a tiny bit.

What exactly is a memory, how is a moment in time stored inside your brain, and why does remembering something slowly rewrite the story of your life?

Earth may seem like the perfect planet for life, but huge parts of our planet are actually hostile deserts of ice, rock, heat or darkness. Even our oceans are mostly barren voids where almost nothing can survive. But as we discover and research more exoplanets, we are beginning to wonder whether there could be worlds that are far better suited for life than Earth ever was, and where ecosystems flourish on a scale we can barely even imagine.

Could there be a Super-Earth that is not just habitable, but truly ideal for life? What would such a world look like, and what kinds of creatures would we find there?

Losing weight is hard, and obesity is one of the unhealthiest things that can happen to your body.    <media:description>Do you have a balanced news diet? Go to https://ground.news/nutshell to see reporting from a variety of sources and perspectives around the world. Subscribe for 40% off their unlimited access Vantage plan through our link.

Support independent science and get something beautiful in return: https://shop.kgs.link/favorites

Sources & further reading:
https://sites.google.com/view/sources-germany-is-over/

Germany is heading towards a population collapse. For decades, birth rates have remained below replacement levels, while people are living longer than ever before. As a result, the population is now rapidly aging and facing a growing imbalance between the number of workers and retirees. This shift is putting pressure not only on Germany's pension system, but also on jobs, healthcare, and the services people rely on every day.

So how did Germany get here and what will be the consequences? Is there a way to avoid this demographic collapse and if so, how?
